Scenario.title: Operation Sharpshooter Healthcare and Public Health Cyber Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex Operation Sharpshooter is a sophisticated cyber attack targeting healthcare and public health organizations. The attackers employ various tactics to gain initial access, execute malware, maintain persistence, escalate privileges, evade defense mechanisms, access credentials, discover sensitive information, move laterally, collect data, exfiltrate data, and disrupt operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Gather information about healthcare and public health organizations

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Device theft, Weak passwords, Improper configuration, Vulnerability, Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Obsolete products, Injection flaws, Custom APIs, Autonomous system numbers(ASNs), SSL Certificates and Attribution, WHOIS records, contacts and history, TCP/IP ports, Public and private cloud, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Malicious insiders, Social engineering, Insufficient physical security controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: External/Removable media, Loss or Theft of equipment, Attrition, Web, Account takeover, Inadequate encryption, Browser-based attack, Application compromise, Open port, Vulnerability exploit, Ransomware, Malware, Man in The Middle(MiTM), Session hijacking, Brute-force attack, Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS), Watering-hole, Compromised and weak credentials, SIM Swap, Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities, Web forms, IP address and IP blocks, Domains and subdomains, Botnet, Credential compromise, Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS), Replay Attack, Cryptanalysis Attacks, Side Channel Attacks, Hardware Trojans, Covert Attacks, Sleep Deprivation Attack, Clock Skewing, Attacks Targeting System Reputation, Data and Command Integrity Attack, Eavesdropping, Command and Control Attacks, Phishing, Impersonations/Spoofing, Improper usage, Dumpster diving, Advanced Persistent Threat(APT), Baiting, Shoulder surfing, Reverse social engineering, Business Email Compromise(BEC), Advanced Fee, Extortion

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.technique_name: Spear Phishing via Compromised Email Account
stage.description: The attackers use spear phishing via a compromised email account to trick employees of healthcare and public health organizations into revealing sensitive information.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers compromise employee devices by having them visit malicious websites.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers use PowerShell to execute malware on compromised devices.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks to run at specific times to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers use WMI to maintain persistence on Windows systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers disable anti-virus software to evade detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers use brute force attacks to obtain login credentials.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan networks for open ports and services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers use remote services to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from local systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate data over alternative protocols to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers cause a Denial of Service to disrupt operations.

Scenario.summary: Operation Sharpshooter is a complex cyber attack targeting healthcare and public health organizations. The attackers use spear phishing, drive-by compromise, PowerShell, scheduled tasks, WMI, exploiting public-facing applications, anti-virus tampering, brute force attacks, network scanning, remote services, collecting data from local systems, exfiltrating data over alternative protocols, and causing a Denial of Service to achieve their objectives.
